content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
March 3, 2016 
Matthew Alvarez 
The Sutton Law Firm 
150 Post Street, Suite 405 
San Francisco, CA 94108 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-16-015 
Dear Mr. Alvarez: 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the lobbying provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the .. Act"). 1 Because your inquiry is general in nature, we are treating it as a 
request for informal assistance. 2 
QUESTION 
Does the exception to the definition of a "placement agent" under Section 82047.3(b) apply 
to managers of real estate funds who spend one-third or more of their time managing fund 
properties and projects? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. The exception under Section 82047.3(b) applies to managers of real estate funds as 
described below. 
FACTS 
Your law firm represents several investment firms that have real estate funds in which the 
California Public Employees' Retirement System ("CalPERS"), the California State Teachers' 
Retirement System ("CalSTRS"), or the University of California Retirement System ("UCRS") are 
considering, or have made investments in real estate funds. 
Unlike the partners or employees who manage traditional investment funds, the employees 
and partners working on these real estate funds do not buy and sell stocks or bonds. Instead, these 
real estate fund employees oversee the selection of real estate projects in which the fund may invest, 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission arc contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Tille 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
2 Informal assistance docs not provide the requestor with immunity provided by an opinion or formal written 
advice. (Section 84113; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) 


File No. I-16-015 
Page No. 2 
review the approval process for pending development projects, and manage the ultimate real estate 
assets. 
These real estate funds have a partner or employee who is responsible for identifying 
potential real estate investments, conducting "due diligence" on the viability of a particular real 
estate development project, seeking land use entitlements for the project from the applicable local 
jurisdiction, and overseeing the management of the properties in the funds. 
In addition to these duties, the firm may ask these partners and employees to meet with 
representatives of CalPERS, CaISTRS, or UCRS to discuss the possibility that the state pension 
plan make an investment in the real estate fund. These duties take up far less of the employee's time 
in any given month than the employee's core functions or duties. You wish to know whether the 
exception under Section 82047.3(b) applies to managers of real estate funds who spend one-third or 
more of their time managing fund properties and projects. 
ANALYSIS 
Under Section 82047.3 a placement agent is defined broadly as "an individual hired, 
engaged, or retained by, or serving for the benefit of or on behalf of, an external manager, or on 
behalf of another placement agent, who acts or has acted for compensation as a finder, solicitor, 
marketer, consultant, broker, or other intermediary in connection with the offer or sale of the 
securities, assets, or services of an external manager to a state public retirement system in California 
or an investment vehicle, either directly or indirectly." 
There are limited exceptions to the definition of "placement agent." One exception in 
Section 82047.3(b) states: 
"Notwithstanding subdivision (a) [the placement agent definition], an 
individual who is an employee, officer, director, equityholder, partner, member, 
or trustee of an external manager and who spends one-third or more of his or her 
time, during a calendar year, managing the securities or assets owned, controlled, 
invested, or held by the external manager is not a placement agent." 
Your facts indicate that real estate fund managers oversee real estate investments by 
identifying and selecting the projects to invest in, reviewing the approval of pending projects, 
seeking land use entitlements for the projects, and managing the real estate assets. 
Accordingly, we find that these real estate fund managers manage "assets owned, controlled, 
invested, or held" by the real estate fund. Therefore, the exception under Section 82047.3(b) applies 
to these real estate fund managers you describe, and these persons would not be considered 
placement agents under the Act provided they spend one-third or more of their time during a 
calendar year managing the assets. 


ER:jgl 
File No. I-16-015 
Page No. 3 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Emelyn Rodriguez 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 3

described below.

QUESTION: Does the exception to the definition of a "placement agent" under Section 82047.3(b) apply 
to managers of real estate funds who spend one-third or more of their time managing fund 
properties and projects?

CONCLUSION: Yes. The exception under Section 82047.3(b) applies to managers of real estate funds as 
described below.
